title: Multiplicity One Conjecture in Min-max theory
speaker: Xin Zhou
affiliation: University of California, Santa Barbara; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: I will present a proof with some substantial details of the Multiplicity One Conjecture in Min-max theory, raised by Marques and Neves. It says that in a closed manifold of dimension between 3 and 7 with a bumpy metric, the min-max minimal hypersurfaces associated with the volume spectrum introduced by Gromov, Guth, Marques-Neves are all two-sided and have multiplicity one.

proof_stages.label: Linear analogy and area variational setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames multiplicity one as a nonlinear analogue of generic simplicity of Laplace eigenvalues, then sets up minimal hypersurfaces as critical points of the area functional and recalls classical existence and Yau's infinitude problem.

proof_stages.label: Cycle-space Morse theory and volume spectrum
proof_stages.summary: The min-max framework is built on the mod-2 cycle space, whose Almgren topology gives a polynomial cohomology generator used to define k-sweepouts and hence the k-th volume spectrum by minimizing maximal area.

proof_stages.label: Min-max realization with multiplicities
proof_stages.summary: The Almgren-Pitts-Schoen-Simon-Marques-Neves theorem realizes each volume-spectrum value as a finite sum of areas of smooth embedded minimal hypersurfaces with integer multiplicities and total Morse index at most k, while compactness explains why these multiplicities can occur.

proof_stages.label: Multiplicity One theorem and generic hypotheses
proof_stages.summary: The Multiplicity One Conjecture/Theorem is stated for bumpy metrics, after defining two-sidedness, the Jacobi operator, Morse index, and bumpy metrics, with prior one-parameter and Allen-Cahn results placed as context.

proof_stages.label: Weighted Morse-index application via bumpy approximation
proof_stages.summary: As an application, the weighted Morse-index upper bound for arbitrary metrics is reduced to perturbing the metric to bumpy ones, applying multiplicity one and the known index bound, and preparing to pass to a compactness limit.

proof_stages.label: Compactness transfer of negative directions
proof_stages.summary: Sharp compactness and localization away from finitely many bad points allow negative second-variation directions on the limiting hypersurface to be lifted to the sheets of the approximating hypersurfaces, giving m·index≤k for two-sided limits and (m/2)·index≤k for one-sided limits.

proof_stages.label: PMC perturbation strategy for multiplicity one
proof_stages.summary: The proposed proof strategy approximates area by Lagrange-multiplier prescribed-mean-curvature functionals whose one-sided maximum principle breaks the usual strong maximum principle responsible for stacked multiplicity, forcing multiplicity-one or controlled touching limits.
